content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates some perceptual accuracy but it is uneven and largely content-tracking until the critical turn 10. In turns 2-8, the coach asks questions appropriate to the topic but never names how the client is showing up — no read on the careful, distanced, slightly intellectualized stance, no read on the linguistic caveats ('I'm not sure if that's wisdom or resignation'), no naming of the managing/controlled quality even though the client opened by literally saying 'I've been managing my emotions for years.' Turn 8 reframes content but does not name a somatic or stance read. Turn 10 is where perceptual accuracy clearly appears: the coach catches the walk-back ('I shouldn't have said that. Forget I said that.') and explicitly refuses to let it slide ('I'm not going to forget that'). That is a real read of the retract-and-minimize move, named in the moment, and it changes the conversation — the client even confirms 'I was testing whether you would.' But this is one timely read in an otherwise content-driven arc; the rush-signature / managing-signature reads that were active from turn 1 were missed. One strong, timely read plus several missed opportunities lands squarely in 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Body-oriented question but no read named; reasonable opening, not yet perceptual."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Tracks content (duration of the hum) without naming the distanced, observing-from-outside quality the client is showing."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Jumps to fear-content question; misses the controlled, carefully-hedged stance and the 'I've done the work' identity-armor signal."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Notices the wisdom/resignation distinction the client made — a light meta-observation, but it's a content reframe rather than a stance/somatic read."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the retract-and-minimize ('forget I said that') in the moment and explicitly names that he won't move past it. Timely, specific read that changes the conversation."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "The rush/managing signature was visible from turn 1 ('managing my emotions for years') and in the careful hedging throughout turns 5-9, but was never named. The one strong read (catching the walk-back at turn 10) is what lifts this above a 1."
}
